entities.name: TEAK
entities.description: Tectona grandis L., a tree component in the agroforestry system

entities.name: BAEL
entities.description: Aegle marmelos L., a tree component in the agroforestry system

entities.name: CHICKPEA
entities.description: Cicer arietinum L., a crop grown within the agroforestry system

entities.name: LINSEED
entities.description: Linum usitatissimum L., a crop grown within the agroforestry system

entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: A crop used in the cropping system with chickpea

entities.name: MAIZE
entities.description: A crop used in the cropping system with linseed

entities.name: CONSERVATION AGRICULTURE
entities.description: Agricultural practices aimed at protecting the soil and environment

entities.name: AGROFORESTRY
entities.description: Integration of trees and crops on the same land

entities.name: CONVENTIONAL TILLAGE
entities.description: Standard tillage practice used in the study

entities.name: MINIMUM TILLAGE
entities.description: Reduced tillage practice used to study soil and crop impact

entities.name: RESIDUE RETENTION
entities.description: The practice of leaving crop residues on the soil surface

entities.name: RANDOMIZED BLOCK DESIGN
entities.description: The experimental design used for the study

entities.name: GROWTH RATE
entities.description: The rate at which the tree and crop components grow

entities.name: YIELD
entities.description: The amount of crop produced per unit area

entities.name: SOIL PROPERTIES
entities.description: Characteristics of the soil including organic carbon and nutrients

entities.name: PLANT HEIGHT
entities.description: A growth parameter measured for linseed and chickpea

entities.name: DRY MATTER ACCUMULATION
entities.description: A growth parameter measured for linseed and chickpea

entities.name: SOIL ORGANIC CARBON CONTENT
entities.description: The amount of organic carbon present in the soil

entities.name: NUTRIENT AVAILABILITY
entities.description: The availability of N, P, K, S, Zn, Fe, Mn, and Cu in the soil

entities.name: BUNDELKHAND REGION
entities.description: The geographical area where the case study was conducted

entities.name: WINTER SEASON
entities.description: The rabi season during which the study was carried out

relationships.description: Includes teak as a tree component
Includes bael as a tree component
Significantly boosted the yields of chickpea and linseed
Improved soil organic carbon content by 24-39%
Enhanced nutrient availability when combined with residue retention
Enhanced nutrient availability when combined with minimum tillage
Significantly enhanced plant height when combined with residue retention
Significantly enhanced dry matter accumulation when combined with residue retention
Contributed to higher productivity in the agroforestry system
